stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">1 square</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">2 squares</text><text x="60" y="72" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">3 squares</text><text x="60" y="90" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">next = ?</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: The distractors keep the same visual style but use lower or higher square counts to catch undercounting.
items.solution_rule: The square count increases by one across the sequence, so the next panel needs four filled squares.

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">Rotate 90° each step</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">choose the next arrow</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: Distractors reuse prior orientations or partial rotations to test whether the rotation rule was tracked consistently.
items.solution_rule: The arrow rotates 90 degrees clockwise on each step, so the next state is the 270-degree orientation.

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">Dot moves clockwise</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">around the square</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: Distractors place the dot on earlier positions in the cycle or repeat the start position.
items.solution_rule: The dot moves clockwise around the four corners of the square, so the missing position is bottom-left.

asset.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><polygon points="60,24 92,88 28,88" fill="#111111" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="2" stroke-linejoin="round"/></svg>

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">Outline -> Filled</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">apply the same rule</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: Distractors preserve the wrong shape or fail to apply the fill transformation consistently.
items.solution_rule: The left pair changes an outlined circle into a filled circle; applying the same rule to an outlined square yields a filled square.

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">Find the same</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">upright L-shape</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: The distractors mirror or rotate the target shape so they look similar without preserving the original orientation.
items.solution_rule: The target is the same L-shape orientation shown in the stem, and only option A contains that orientation unchanged.

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">Choose the same shape</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">after rotation only</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: Distractors mirror the shape or keep the original orientation, which is visually close but rule-inconsistent.
items.solution_rule: The base polyomino is rotated without mirroring, and only option B matches a pure 90-degree rotation.

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">Fill the missing</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">corner piece</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: Distractors supply pieces with the wrong footprint, missing the horizontal foot or overfilling the gap.
items.solution_rule: The gap in the square needs one long vertical segment plus a short horizontal foot, which matches the L-shaped piece.

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">Which option is</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">different?</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: The distractors all preserve simple reflection symmetry, so the odd one out is the only asymmetric pattern.
items.solution_rule: Three options are mirror-symmetric around at least one axis, while option D breaks that symmetry with an offset cluster.

asset.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">32</text></svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">34</text></svg>

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">2, 4, 8, 16, ?</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: Distractors include nearby even numbers and repeated powers to catch skipped or partial doubling.
items.solution_rule: Each term doubles the previous term, so the next value after 16 is 32.

asset.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">8</text></svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">10</text></svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">12</text></svg>

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">2  4</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">3  6</text><text x="60" y="72" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">5  ?</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: Distractors keep the numbers close but break the row-doubling rule by under- or over-shooting the product.
items.solution_rule: In each row the second number is double the first, so the row starting with 5 must end with 10.

asset.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">4</text></svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">5</text></svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">6</text></svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="16" y="20" width="88" height="80" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="4"/><text x="60" y="72" font-size="26" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="700" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">7</text></svg>

stem.svg_markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 120 120" fill="none"><rect x="10" y="14" width="100" height="92" rx="8" fill="#F8FAFC" stroke="#111111" stroke-width="3"/><text x="60" y="36" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">triangle = 2</text><text x="60" y="54" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">square = 3</text><text x="60" y="72" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">circle = 4</text><text x="60" y="90" font-size="13" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-weight="600" text-anchor="middle" fill="#111111">triangle + circle = ?</text></svg>

items.distractor_logic: Distractors reuse one of the symbol values or add an extra step to test whether the mapping was applied cleanly.
items.solution_rule: The stem assigns values triangle=2 and circle=4, so adding them yields 6.

items.distractor_logic: Distractors represent common under-counts that miss overlapping or edge triangles in the figure.
items.solution_rule: The figure can be decomposed into eight distinct small triangles when counted systematically from top to bottom.
